query: I'm using the MS-MARCO dataset to evaluate my RAG system. There is one snapshot of the dataset and I have embedded all of it once. Audit the embeddings for drift.
expected_behavior: Identifies that drift analysis requires two distributions to compare (a baseline cohort and a comparison cohort across a time axis, source axis, model-version axis, or other meaningful dimension) and a single static snapshot has no such second cohort
Declines to compute drift metrics or to produce a drift report for the static single-cohort case rather than fabricating a comparison

Suggests an alternative skill or workflow that actually applies (one-shot retrieval evaluation, chunking-strategy audit, or any other approach that operates on a single dataset rather than requiring two cohorts)
